metadata.title: Apply 3 changes to frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai-dental.com/ui_kits/website/dashboard.html to upgrade the New Appointment drawer with typeahe...

content.data:
All 3 changes applied to `dashboard.html`:

1. **CSS** — 14 new rules (`.search-select`, `.search-results`, `.search-opt`, `.search-ava`, `.field-label-row`, `.field-link`, `.drawer-back`) inserted after `.field-row`, before `/* Toast */`.

2. **PROVIDERS + NewPatientDrawer** — `PROVIDERS` array with 3 providers and a full `NewPatientDrawer` component (first/last name, DOB, phone, email, insurance, minor checkbox with conditional guardian field, consent checkbox, back arrow header, disabled save button) inserted before `NewAppointmentDrawer`.

3. **NewAppointmentDrawer replaced** — Patient field now uses a typeahead `search-select` with avatar initials + name + ID in the dropdown, plus a "+ New patient" link that opens the `NewPatientDrawer` sub-drawer. Provider field uses the same typeahead pattern filtering the `PROVIDERS` array showing name + operatory. On new patient save, auto-selects the patient and returns to the appointment drawer. All other fields unchanged.

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me summarize what I see and ask clarifying questions.

---

Here's my understanding: You want to implement a **full-page overlay/workspace** that opens when clicking an existing appointment card on the dashboard. The design feedback from `image_6c9d39.png` suggests this should be a dedicated clinical workspace — not just a detail drawer — that gives the practitioner room for tooth charts, notes, and treatment history. You want this built in both the **design system prototype** (`ui_kits/website/dashboard.html`) and the **production React app** (`frontend/src/`).

Before I plan this out, I have a few questions:

**[1]**: Which interaction pattern do you want for clicking an appointment card?
- a. **Full-page overlay** — Clicking the card immediately opens a full-page clinical workspace (tooth chart, notes, treatment history, status actions). A "Back to Dashboard" button returns.
- b. **Hybrid (inline expand → full-page)** — Clicking the card first expands it inline to show quick actions (Check-in, Open Chart, Reschedule). Clicking "Open Chart" then triggers the full-page workspace.
- c. **Other** — Describe your preferred flow.

**[2]**: Where should this be built?
- a. **Design system prototype only** (`dashboard.html`) — A static/interactive HTML mock of the full-page workspace.
- b. **Production React app only** (`frontend/src/`) — Wire the appointment card click in the Dashboard to navigate to the existing `Patient360` page (or a new appointment workspace route).
- c. **Both** — Design system prototype first, then production React implementation.

**[3]**: What clinical data should the full-page workspace show? The existing `Patient360` already has tabs for Overview, Appointments, Documents, Insurance, Treatment Plans, Lab Cases, Communications, and Notes. Should the appointment workspace:
- a. **Reuse Patient360** — Navigate to `/patients/:id` with the appointment context pre-loaded (e.g., auto-open the relevant tab).
- b. **New appointment-focused workspace** — A dedicated layout with the appointment at the center: patient header, tooth chart, SOAP notes editor, status actions, and treatment plan — all visible without tabs.
- c. **Other** — Describe what you envision.

Good. Now I have a complete picture. Let me ask the remaining clarifying questions based on your answers:

You said:
1. **c (Other)** for the interaction pattern — can you describe what you want? Just clicking the appointment card opens a full-page overlay focused on that specific appointment?
2. **a** — Design system prototype only (`dashboard.html`)
3. **c (Other)** — Focus on the selected appointment

Let me ask follow-up questions to nail down the full-page workspace content:

**[1]**: For the full-page appointment workspace, what sections should be visible when you click an appointment card (e.g., "Alice Stevens · 08:30 · Recall · 6mo")?
- a. **Compact clinical view** — Appointment header (patient avatar + name + time + procedure + status), tooth chart, and status action buttons (Confirm, Check-in, Start, Complete, No-show, Cancel, Reschedule). Focused on the single appointment.
- b. **Rich clinical view** — Everything in (a) plus a SOAP notes area and treatment history/plan section for that visit.
- c. **Other** — Describe what you want on the page.

**[2]**: For the transition animation when clicking an appointment card, what do you prefer?
- a. **Fade + slide up** — The dashboard fades out and the workspace slides up (400ms ease-out per the design system motion tokens).
- b. **Shared element transition** — The clicked appointment card animates/expands to become the workspace header (the "visual tether" mentioned in the feedback).
- c. **Simple crossfade** — Dashboard fades out, workspace fades in.

**[3]**: Should the "Back to Dashboard" button be:
- a. **Top-left arrow + text** — A back arrow with "Back to Dashboard" label in the workspace header.
- b. **X close button** — Same pattern as the drawer close button, top-right.
- c. **Both** — Back arrow top-left AND X button top-right.

Now I have everything I need. Let me formulate the implementation plan.

---

**Implementation Plan — Full-Page Appointment Workspace (Design System Prototype)**

**Problem Statement:**
When clicking an existing appointment card on the dashboard, the current behavior opens a simple `DetailDrawer` (right-side panel with key-value rows). The goal is to replace this with a full-page overlay workspace focused on the selected appointment, providing a compact clinical view with appointment header, tooth chart, and status action buttons. This is design system prototype work only (`dashboard.html`).

**Requirements:**
- Clicking an appointment card opens a full-page overlay (not a drawer) focused on that appointment
- The workspace shows: appointment header (patient avatar + name + time + procedure + status), tooth chart, and status action buttons (Confirm, Check-in, Start, Complete, No-show, Cancel, Reschedule)
- Transition: fade + slide up (400ms ease-out per design system motion tokens)
- "Back to Dashboard" button: top-left arrow + text label
- Design system prototype only — `dashboard.html` in `ui_kits/website/`
- Follow all design system tokens from `colors_and_type.css` — no raw hex outside existing token values
- Brand voice: authoritative, clinical, calm. No emoji.

**Background:**
- The dashboard currently uses `setDetail(...)` → `DetailDrawer` for appointment card clicks
- The `DetailDrawer` is a generic right-side drawer with key-value rows — also used for patients, invoices, and lab cases
- The `ToothChartTile` component already exists in the design system with FDI numbering and status fills
- The `AppointmentCard` component provides the appointment data shape: `{ id, time, duration, patient_id, patient, provider, chair, kind, status }`
- Status tones are already defined in `AppointmentCard.jsx`: confirmed (green), pending (amber), no_show (red), completed (grey)
- Design system motion: `--duration-slow: 400ms`, `--ease-out: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`
- The `drawer-back` CSS class was already added in the previous change set

**Proposed Solution:**
1. Add CSS for the full-page workspace overlay (`.appt-workspace`, animation keyframes)
2. Create an `AppointmentWorkspace` component that renders the full-page view for a selected appointment
3. Change the `Dashboard` component to use `setActiveAppt(appointment)` instead of `setDetail(...)` for appointment card clicks, rendering `AppointmentWorkspace` when an appointment is selected
4. Keep `DetailDrawer` intact for patients, invoices, and lab cases — only appointment cards change behavior

**Task Breakdown:**

Task 1: Add CSS for the full-page appointment workspace
- Clear objective: Add CSS rules for `.appt-workspace` (full-page overlay), `.appt-ws-header`, `.appt-ws-body`, `.appt-ws-actions`, and a `@keyframes wsEnter` animation (fade + slide up, 400ms)
- Implementation: Insert after the existing `.drawer-back:hover` rule block, before `/* Toast */`. Use design system tokens (`--ease-out`, `--duration-slow`, `--rr-warm-white`, `--rr-parchment`, etc.). The workspace is `position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: 40; background: var(--rr-warm-white)` with the slide-up animation.
- Test: Visual — open dashboard.html, confirm no CSS errors, workspace styles are defined.
- Demo: CSS loads without errors; no visual change yet since no component uses the classes.

Task 2: Create the `AppointmentWorkspace` component
- Clear objective: Build a React component that receives an appointment object and `onClose` callback, rendering a full-page overlay with: (1) a top bar with back-arrow + "Back to Dashboard" label, (2) an appointment header showing patient avatar/initials, patient name, appointment ID, time, duration, procedure, provider, operatory, and status badge, (3) the `ToothChartTile` component, and (4) a row of status action buttons (Confirm, Check-in, Start, Complete, No-show, Cancel, Reschedule).
- Implementation: Insert the component definition before the `Dashboard` component in the `<script type="text/babel">` block. Use the existing `drawer-back` class for the back button. Reuse the status tone map from `AppointmentCard.jsx` for the status badge. The tooth chart is the existing `<ToothChartTile />`. Action buttons use `btn btn-primary btn-md` and `btn btn-ghost btn-md` classes. ESC key closes the workspace.
- Test: Visual — component renders correctly when called with a sample appointment.
- Demo: The `AppointmentWorkspace` component exists and can be rendered.

Task 3: Wire appointment card clicks to the workspace and keep DetailDrawer for other entities
- Clear objective: In the `Dashboard` component, add `activeAppt` state. Change the `AppointmentCard` `onClick` to `setActiveAppt(a)` instead of `setDetail(...)`. Render `{activeAppt && <AppointmentWorkspace appointment={activeAppt} onClose={() => setActiveAppt(null)} />}` alongside the existing `detail && <DetailDrawer ...>` (which still handles patients, invoices, lab cases).
- Implementation: Add `const [activeAppt, setActiveAppt] = React.useState(null);` to Dashboard state. Replace the `onClick` in the `AppointmentCard` map. Add the conditional render near the other overlays.
- Test: Visual — click an appointment card, full-page workspace appears with fade+slide-up. Click "Back to Dashboard", returns. Click a patient card or invoice row, the old DetailDrawer still works.
- Demo: Full end-to-end flow: click appointment → workspace opens with animation → see patient info, tooth chart, action buttons → click back → dashboard returns. Other cards (patients, invoices, lab) still open the DetailDrawer.

Does this plan look good, or would you like me to adjust anything?

content.data:
Clean. All three tasks are complete:

1. **CSS** — 14 rules for `.appt-workspace` full-page overlay with `@keyframes wsEnter` (fade + 16px slide-up, 400ms ease-out). Topbar, hero card, avatar, info, actions — all using design system tokens.

2. **AppointmentWorkspace component** — Full-page overlay with back arrow + "Back to Dashboard" label, hero card (patient avatar/initials, name, procedure/provider/operatory/time details, status badge), action buttons (Confirm, Check in, Start, Complete, No show, Reschedule, Cancel), and the existing `ToothChartTile`. ESC key closes.

3. **Wiring** — Appointment card clicks now call `setActiveAppt(a)` instead of `setDetail(...)`. The `AppointmentWorkspace` renders when `activeAppt` is set. `DetailDrawer` still handles patients, invoices, and lab cases unchanged.

The implementation is already complete — all three tasks were executed in the previous turn. The changes are live in `dashboard.html`:

- **Line 70–83**: Workspace CSS (`.appt-workspace`, animation, topbar, hero, actions)
- **Line 465–511**: `AppointmentWorkspace` component (full-page overlay with back button, patient hero card, status actions, tooth chart)
- **Line 520**: `activeAppt` state added to Dashboard
- **Line 571**: Appointment card clicks wired to `setActiveAppt(a)`
- **Line 645**: `AppointmentWorkspace` rendered when `activeAppt` is set

You can test it now by opening `dashboard.html` in a browser and clicking any appointment card. Is there something else you'd like me to add or adjust?

Done. Here's the hybrid 2-step flow:

**Step 1 — Inline expand**: Click any appointment card → card highlights with a steel-blue border and elevated shadow, and a quick-action bar appears below with **Check in**, **Open Chart**, and **Reschedule** buttons. Click the same card again to collapse.

**Step 2 — Full-page workspace**: Click **Open Chart** → the dashboard fades away and the full-page appointment workspace slides up (400ms ease-out) showing the patient hero card, status action buttons, and tooth chart. "Back to Dashboard" arrow returns to the dashboard.

Files modified:
- `AppointmentCard.jsx` — accepts `expanded` prop for visual highlight state
- `dashboard.html` — CSS for `.appt-quick-actions`, `expandedApptId` state, inline quick actions rendering, and the existing `AppointmentWorkspace` full-page overlay

Structure is correct: `appt-ws-backdrop` → `appt-workspace` → topbar + body → close both divs.

"Using a full-page transition when clicking an existing appointment card in image_6c9d39.png is a bold choice that signals a shift from "Overview" to "Deep Work."While the Full-Page Overlay is excellent for creating a dedicated workspace, consider how it changes the user's mental model compared to a "New Appointment" action.The Full-Page Transition StrategyIf you go with this approach, the dashboard should feel like it's "folding away" to bring the patient's clinical file to the front.Pros: It provides maximum real estate for the Tooth Chart, clinical notes, and treatment history seen in your "Today's appointments" section. It's ideal for practitioners who need to enter significant amounts of data during the visit.The "Back" Experience: Since this is a full-page shift, you must provide a very clear "Back to Dashboard" or "Close" button (usually in the top left) so the user doesn't feel "lost" in a new app.Alternative: The "Contextual Expansion"Since you are a fan of minimalist design, consider if a full-page shift might be too heavy for a quick status check.The "Hybrid" Approach: When clicking an appointment card (like "Alice Stevens"), the card itself could expand vertically to reveal quick actions (e.g., "Check-in," "Open Chart," "Reschedule").The Workflow: If they click "Open Chart," then you trigger the full-page transition. This prevents a "jarring" experience if the user only wanted to see a quick detail.Comparison for Existing AppointmentsEffectUX ImpactBest Use CaseFull-PageHigh FocusComprehensive clinical charting and note-taking.Right DrawerHigh EfficiencyQuick updates (e.g., changing status from "Pending" to "Confirmed").Inline ExpandLow FrictionViewing quick procedural notes without leaving the list.Visual Tip for "image_6c9d39.png"If you use the Full-Page Overlay, try a Shared Element Transition. For example, when you click the "Alice Stevens" box, that specific blue "AS" icon could animate and move to its new position on the dedicated workspace page. This creates a visual "tether" so the user understands exactly where they just moved.Does the "Full-Page" workspace feel like the right home for the detailed clinical data you're managing, or do you want users to be able to jump between appointments quickly?" check the frontend/src to see the structure of the box. also make sure find the components from the system design and create it!

content.data:
---
name: rockyridge-dental-design
description: Use this skill to generate well-branded interfaces, components, and assets for Rockyridge Dental AI — the dental practice management product of Rockyridge AI Solutions. Provides shadcn-shape primitives layered with the parent Rockyridge brand language (Navy / Steel / Warm-white palette, Montserrat + Inter typography, sweep-fill button motif). Default light theme for clinical surfaces; dark theme reserved for the login portal. Use for production runtime code, throwaway HTML prototypes, marketing pages, and dental-domain composites (patient cards, tooth charts, lab pipelines, treatment plans).
user-invocable: true
---

## When to invoke

- Implementing or styling any UI inside the Rockyridge Dental AI app (`frontend/src/` of the dental-api repo).
- Generating a static HTML prototype, marketing page, or pitch artifact for the dental product.
- Adding a new shadcn-shape component and needing the brand-correct token bindings.
- Designing a dental-domain composite (PatientCard, AppointmentCard, ToothChart, LabPipeline, KpiTile, etc.).
- Resolving a design ambiguity ("what colour is the focus ring?", "which font for KPI numbers?", "is this card the right radius?").

## How to use

1. **Read `README.md` first.** It documents brand voice, component library, file layout, and the design lineage (shadcn → RR brand, in that order).
2. **Read `colors_and_type.css`.** Every token is here. If a value isn't tokenised, use the closest existing one — do *not* invent new colours, radii, or font sizes.
3. **Browse `preview/` for the component you need.** Each preview is a self-contained HTML page that imports `../colors_and_type.css` and demonstrates the component in default + variant states. Copy the markup as a starting point.
4. **Check `ui_kits/website/` for full-bleed compositions.** PascalCase JSX files mirror the parent `rockyridgeai.com/ui_kits/website/` layout. `dashboard.html` (light) and `login.html` (dark) are the canonical product prototypes.
5. **Output rules:**
   - For *production code* (live React app), import the runtime primitives at `frontend/src/components/ui/<name>.tsx` — they already consume these tokens. Do not reach into the design_system folder for runtime imports.
   - For *throwaway artifacts* (HTML mocks, slides, pitch decks), copy the relevant preview as a starting point, swap the `../colors_and_type.css` link to a relative path that resolves, and stay strict to the token names.
6. **Brand voice:** authoritative, clinical, calm. Never emoji. "We → you" framing. Definite article on system parts (*The Schedule*, *The Roster*, *The Lab*).

## What NOT to do

- Do not introduce new colours, fonts, or shadows outside `colors_and_type.css`.
- Do not flip to dark theme for clinical surfaces. Login portal is the only dark-theme exception.
- Do not use emoji. Do not use filled icons. Do not use playful / spring easing.
- Do not bypass `--primary` / `--foreground` / `--border` in favour of raw hex values inside components — keep the indirection so the dark theme works.

---
name: rockyridge-dental-design
description: Use this skill to generate well-branded interfaces, components, and assets for Rockyridge Dental AI — the dental practice management product of Rockyridge AI Solutions. Provides shadcn-shape primitives layered with the parent Rockyridge brand language (Navy / Steel / Warm-white palette, Montserrat + Inter typography, sweep-fill button motif). Default light theme for clinical surfaces; dark theme reserved for the login portal. Use for production runtime code, throwaway HTML prototypes, marketing pages, and dental-domain composites (patient cards, tooth charts, lab pipelines, treatment plans).
user-invocable: true
---

## When to invoke

- Implementing or styling any UI inside the Rockyridge Dental AI app (`frontend/src/` of the dental-api repo).
- Generating a static HTML prototype, marketing page, or pitch artifact for the dental product.
- Adding a new shadcn-shape component and needing the brand-correct token bindings.
- Designing a dental-domain composite (PatientCard, AppointmentCard, ToothChart, LabPipeline, KpiTile, etc.).
- Resolving a design ambiguity ("what colour is the focus ring?", "which font for KPI numbers?", "is this card the right radius?").

## How to use

1. **Read `README.md` first.** It documents brand voice, component library, file layout, and the design lineage (shadcn → RR brand, in that order).
2. **Read `colors_and_type.css`.** Every token is here. If a value isn't tokenised, use the closest existing one — do *not* invent new colours, radii, or font sizes.
3. **Browse `preview/` for the component you need.** Each preview is a self-contained HTML page that imports `../colors_and_type.css` and demonstrates the component in default + variant states. Copy the markup as a starting point.
4. **Check `ui_kits/website/` for full-bleed compositions.** PascalCase JSX files mirror the parent `rockyridgeai.com/ui_kits/website/` layout. `dashboard.html` (light) and `login.html` (dark) are the canonical product prototypes.
5. **Output rules:**
   - For *production code* (live React app), import the runtime primitives at `frontend/src/components/ui/<name>.tsx` — they already consume these tokens. Do not reach into the design_system folder for runtime imports.
   - For *throwaway artifacts* (HTML mocks, slides, pitch decks), copy the relevant preview as a starting point, swap the `../colors_and_type.css` link to a relative path that resolves, and stay strict to the token names.
6. **Brand voice:** authoritative, clinical, calm. Never emoji. "We → you" framing. Definite article on system parts (*The Schedule*, *The Roster*, *The Lab*).

## What NOT to do

- Do not introduce new colours, fonts, or shadows outside `colors_and_type.css`.
- Do not flip to dark theme for clinical surfaces. Login portal is the only dark-theme exception.
- Do not use emoji. Do not use filled icons. Do not use playful / spring easing.
- Do not bypass `--primary` / `--foreground` / `--border` in favour of raw hex values inside components — keep the indirection so the dark theme works.

# Rockyridge Dental AI — Design System

## Overview

**Rockyridge Dental AI** is a vertical product of **Rockyridge AI Solutions** — a sovereign clinical platform for denturists, dentists, and dental clinic operators. The brand inherits Rockyridge AI's *Systems Thinking* philosophy and visual language, then layers a clinical UI vocabulary on top: shadcn/ui-shape primitives, calm light-theme surfaces, and dental-domain composites (tooth charts, lab pipelines, treatment plans, recall workflows).

This design system is the single source of truth for every visual decision in the product. It is a sibling of `rockyridgeai.com/` and was written so that anyone — designer, engineer, or AI agent — can produce on-brand UI without re-litigating type, colour, or spacing.

### Design lineage

| Layer | Source | What it contributes |
|------|--------|---------------------|
| Brand | `rockyridgeai.com/` | Navy / Steel / Warm-white palette · Montserrat + Inter typography · sweep-fill button motif · "we → you" voice |
| Component shape | [shadcn/ui](https://ui.shadcn.com) | HSL token shape · semantic naming (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--muted`, …) · Radix primitives mental model |
| Domain | Rockyridge Dental AI | Patient · Provider · Schedule · Lab · Treatment Plan · Insurance composites |

We start from shadcn's component vocabulary and layer the Rockyridge brand on top — *not* the reverse. That ordering matters: it means every shadcn component drops into our codebase against `colors_and_type.css` and inherits the brand without bespoke styling.

---

## Brand wordmark

| Element | Treatment |
|---------|-----------|
| Mark    | `RR` lettermark — `assets/RR_logo_blue.svg` (light bg) · `assets/RR_logo_white.svg` (dark bg) |
| Brand line | **ROCKYRIDGE** · Montserrat 800 · 0.08em tracking · uppercase |
| Sub line   | **DENTAL AI** · Montserrat 400 · 2.1px tracking · uppercase |

The wordmark is locked to the mark with 12px gap. Never recolour, distort, add a glow, or place on a busy photo. Reference: `preview/logo.html`, `.rr-wordmark` utility in `colors_and_type.css`.

The parent brand uses `ROCKYRIDGE / AI SOLUTIONS`. The dental product corrects to `ROCKYRIDGE / DENTAL AI` — same display family, same lockup geometry, only the sub line changes.

---

## CONTENT FUNDAMENTALS

### Tone & voice
- **Authoritative, calm, clinical.** The product speaks like a trusted senior practitioner — measured, precise, never breathless. Borrowed directly from the parent brand and sharpened for healthcare context.
- **"We → you" framing.** *We* (Rockyridge) build the engine; *you* (the clinic) run your practice on top of it.
- **No hype.** Never "world-class," "cutting-edge," "AI-powered." Always specific: "schedules 240 patients/day with no double-booking," "exports CSV to PMS," "OHIP code 23311 lookup."
- **Definite article on system names.** *The Schedule. The Roster. The Lab.* — system parts are named, capitalised, treated as proper nouns.

### Casing
- Section labels / overlines: `ALL CAPS · 0.15EM TRACKING`
- Section headlines: Title Case for product statements, sentence case for descriptive copy
- CTAs: Title Case (e.g., "Schedule a Demo", "Open Patient Chart")
- Body: sentence case

### Emoji usage
**None.** Not in the marketing site, not in the app, not in toasts or empty states. The product does not use emoji.

### Copy examples
- ✅ *"Architecting Sovereign Clinical Systems."*
- ✅ *"Your patient roster is either a liability or a competitive edge."*
- ✅ *"Open the chart. The history is already there."*
- ❌ *"AI-powered scheduling for the modern dentist!"*
- ❌ *"Welcome back :) Let's get you scheduled today."*

---

## VISUAL FOUNDATIONS

### Theme strategy
- **Light theme is the default** for every clinical / admin / patient-facing surface. Operators look at this UI for 8+ hours; warm-white reduces eye strain.
- **Dark theme is reserved** for the **login portal** and any marketing hero pulled from the parent rockyridgeai.com aesthetic. Triggered by adding `.dark` to `<html>` or `<body>`. Component primitives auto-adapt because every colour resolves through `hsl(var(--…))`.
- We do **not** offer a user-facing theme toggle inside the app. The chart and the schedule are clinical instruments — predictable surface colour matters more than personalisation.

### Colour
- **Primary** — Steel Blue `#3A7FBD`. Single action colour. CTAs, links, focus rings, primary chart series.
- **Secondary / Brand** — Navy `#0A192F`. Used for the sidebar, login portal, and marketing hero. Authority colour.
- **Surface** — Warm White `#FAF9F6` (page) · `#FFFFFF` (cards) · Off-White `#F5F2EC` (subtle wash). No greys-on-greys.
- **Ink** — `#1C2333` body · `#3D4D61` secondary · `#4A5568` muted.
- **Semantic** — Success `#2A7D4F` · Warning `#B45309` · Error `#9B2335` · Info = Steel.

Dark sections use `#060F1E` / `#0A192F` with warm-white text. Never grey or pure-black backgrounds.

### Typography
- **Display** — `Montserrat` 600/700/800/900. Headlines, hero, big numbers, KPI tiles. Tight tracking on display sizes (`-0.03em`).
- **UI / Body** — `Inter` 300/400/500/600/700. Every label, button, body paragraph, table cell, form field.
- **Mono** — `JetBrains Mono` 400/500/600. Patient IDs, tooth numbers, lot numbers, codes, durations. Anything that must be a fixed-width clinical reference.

The parent rockyridgeai.com README mentions Playfair + Lora as the *aspirational* serif set; in practice the live site renders with Montserrat + Inter. The dental sub-brand standardises on the live set so engineering has a single typography contract to ship.

### Spacing & layout
- Generous whitespace. Section vertical padding never less than `64px` desktop / `40px` mobile.
- Max content width `1200px`. Text columns capped at `68ch`.
- Grid: 12-column, `24px` gutters. Three-pillar / three-KPI layouts always equal thirds.
- Sidebar fixed left (240px expanded / 64px icon-only). Top bar fixed top (`64px`). No fixed right rail.

### Motion
- `250ms` ease-out for hover / state changes; `400ms` ease-out for page-level reveals.
- Fade + 16px upward drift for section entry.
- Sweep-fill button: `320ms` ease-out, X translate `-101% → 0`.
- No bouncy / spring / playful easing. No spinning loaders — use `Skeleton` for indeterminate state.

### Component shape
- Default border radius `6px`. Pills `999px`. Sharp section blocks `0`. Avoid the over-rounded "consumer-app" feel.
- Card: white bg, `1px solid var(--rr-parchment)`, `shadow-sm` default, `shadow-md` on hover. `32px` padding desktop.
- Inputs: white bg, `1.5px solid var(--rr-mist)`, focus → `1.5px steel-500` + `3px steel-100` ring.
- Shadows are navy-tinted, never pure black (`rgba(10,25,47,…)`).

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me summarize what I see and ask clarifying questions.

---

Here's my understanding: You want to implement a **full-page overlay/workspace** that opens when clicking an existing appointment card on the dashboard. The design feedback from `image_6c9d39.png` suggests this should be a dedicated clinical workspace — not just a detail drawer — that gives the practitioner room for tooth charts, notes, and treatment history. You want this built in both the **design system prototype** (`ui_kits/website/dashboard.html`) and the **production React app** (`frontend/src/`).

Before I plan this out, I have a few questions:

**[1]**: Which interaction pattern do you want for clicking an appointment card?
- a. **Full-page overlay** — Clicking the card immediately opens a full-page clinical workspace (tooth chart, notes, treatment history, status actions). A "Back to Dashboard" button returns.
- b. **Hybrid (inline expand → full-page)** — Clicking the card first expands it inline to show quick actions (Check-in, Open Chart, Reschedule). Clicking "Open Chart" then triggers the full-page workspace.
- c. **Other** — Describe your preferred flow.

**[2]**: Where should this be built?
- a. **Design system prototype only** (`dashboard.html`) — A static/interactive HTML mock of the full-page workspace.
- b. **Production React app only** (`frontend/src/`) — Wire the appointment card click in the Dashboard to navigate to the existing `Patient360` page (or a new appointment workspace route).
- c. **Both** — Design system prototype first, then production React implementation.

**[3]**: What clinical data should the full-page workspace show? The existing `Patient360` already has tabs for Overview, Appointments, Documents, Insurance, Treatment Plans, Lab Cases, Communications, and Notes. Should the appointment workspace:
- a. **Reuse Patient360** — Navigate to `/patients/:id` with the appointment context pre-loaded (e.g., auto-open the relevant tab).
- b. **New appointment-focused workspace** — A dedicated layout with the appointment at the center: patient header, tooth chart, SOAP notes editor, status actions, and treatment plan — all visible without tabs.
- c. **Other** — Describe what you envision.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Common Commands

Local dev (SQLite, auto-syncs schema, runs on port 8001):
```bash
./run_local.sh
```

Manual run (PORT defaults to 8000; `run_api.py` is the entrypoint that always binds to PORT):
```bash
DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///./dental_clinic.db python scripts/sync_db.py   # init + seed
uv run python run_api.py                                              # or: uvicorn api.main:app --reload --port 8001
```

Tests (pytest config in `pyproject.toml`, `testpaths = ["tests"]`):
```bash
uv run pytest                              # full suite
uv run pytest tests/test_api.py            # single file
uv run pytest tests/test_api.py::test_x    # single test
uv run pytest -k slot                      # by keyword
```

Tests use an **in-memory SQLite** DB seeded by `tests/conftest.py` — no real DB needed. The `client` fixture overrides `get_db` and seeds the `default` clinic; `client_market_mall` additionally seeds the `market-mall-denture` clinic (providers 101/102, busy blocks, service 700).

DB migration for existing PG instances:
```bash
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://... python scripts/migrate_add_clinics.py
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://... python scripts/migrate_clinic_contact_fields.py
```

Deployment target is Google Cloud Run — see `DEPLOY_GOOGLE_CLOUD.md`. Cloud Run requires the Unix-socket DSN form: `postgresql://USER:PASS@/DB?host=/cloudsql/PROJECT:REGION:INSTANCE`.

## Architecture

**Single FastAPI app (`api/main.py`) with SQLAlchemy ORM.** The DB is the source of truth; there is no external calendar sync (despite the `calendar_event_id` column and `/api/calendar/*` endpoint names — these are vestigial naming).

### Multi-tenancy (critical)

Every request is scoped by clinic via the `X-Clinic-Id` header (default: `"default"`). This is enforced by the `get_clinic` dependency (`api/main.py:43`), which 404s if the clinic row doesn't exist. **Every query in the API filters by `clinic_id`** — when adding endpoints or queries, you must scope by `clinic.id` or you'll leak data across tenants. Per-clinic config (timezone, working hours, address, contact phone, booking notification email) lives on the `Clinic` row, not in env vars.

Tests reflect this: the `client` fixture seeds the `default` clinic before any request can succeed.

### Slot computation

`tools/slot_utils.get_available_slots` computes availability **from the DB** by intersecting:
- Per-clinic working hours (`hour_start`, `hour_end`) and timezone
- `ProviderBusyBlock` rows (recurring weekly *unavailable* windows — note: busy blocks mean UNAVAILABLE, not available)
- Existing `Appointment` rows with status in `{SCHEDULED, CONFIRMED, PENDING_SYNC, PENDING}`

The same "active statuses" set is used for conflict detection in create/reschedule (`api/main.py:319`, `api/main.py:916`). When changing status semantics, update both the slot computation and the conflict checks together.

### Background notifications

`POST /api/calendar/events`, `PUT /appointments/{id}/cancel`, and `PUT /appointments/{id}/reschedule` schedule **FastAPI `BackgroundTasks`** (in-process, not a queue) for:
- Twilio SMS to the patient (`clients/sms_client.py`) — toggled by `SEND_BOOKING_SMS`, delayed by `SMS_DELAY_SECONDS`.
- SMTP email to the clinic on new bookings (`clients/email_client.py`) — recipient resolved by `resolve_booking_notification_recipient`: `BOOKING_NOTIFICATION_TO` env var overrides the per-clinic `clinic.booking_notification_email` (use the env var only for testing; leave it unset in prod).

Notifications must never raise back to the request handler — they're best-effort and logged on failure.

### Database connection

`database/connection.py` resolves `DATABASE_URL` in this priority order: `POSTGRES_URL` → `POSTGRES_PRISMA_URL` → `POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING` → `DATABASE_URL` → `sqlite:////tmp/dental_clinic.db`. It also normalizes `postgres://` → `postgresql://` and strips Supabase-only `supa=` query params. `init_db()` is called from the FastAPI lifespan, so tables are created on startup; failures are logged but don't crash the app.

### Resilient entrypoint

`run_api.py` always binds to `PORT` even if `api.main` fails to import — it falls back to a stub app that only serves `/health`. This is intentional so Cloud Run / Railway health checks pass and you can debug a misconfigured deploy via logs rather than a crash loop. Don't "fix" this by removing the try/except.

## Design System (mandatory for all frontend/UI work)

**Before writing, modifying, or reviewing any frontend code** (`frontend/src/`, HTML prototypes, marketing pages), you MUST read and follow the Rockyridge Dental AI design system:

1. Read `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai-dental.com/SKILL.md` — it defines when and how to apply the brand.
2. Read `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai-dental.com/README.md` — brand voice, component library, file layout.
3. Read `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai-dental.com/colors_and_type.css` — all design tokens live here. Never invent new colours, radii, or font sizes.
4. Browse `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai-dental.com/preview/` for component markup references.
5. Check `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai-dental.com/ui_kits/website/` for full-page compositions.

Key rules:
- Use `--primary` / `--foreground` / `--border` CSS custom properties — never raw hex values in components.
- Light theme for all clinical surfaces. Dark theme is only for the login portal.
- No emoji, no filled icons, no playful/spring easing.
- Production React code imports from `frontend/src/components/ui/` (which already consumes these tokens). Never import from the `design_system/` folder at runtime.
- Brand voice: authoritative, clinical, calm. Definite article on system parts (The Schedule, The Roster, The Lab).

## Things to know

- **Ignore existing styling in `frontend/src/`.** The current design/CSS in the frontend app is not authoritative. Always follow the Design System section above — do not preserve or match the existing look and feel in `frontend/src/`.
- **Ignore `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai.com/` and `frontend/design_system/dental-pms.v1/`.** Only use `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai-dental.com/` as the design source.
- Provider was renamed from "Doctor"; the `Provider` model is generic (denturist, doctor, assistant, etc.) and `provider.title` + `provider.name` are joined for display (`"Dr Smith"`).
- Default timezone fallback throughout is `America/Edmonton`.
- `tmp/` is an archive of older docs/frontend/scripts — don't add new code there.
- The empty `services/` directory is a leftover; business logic currently lives in `api/main.py` and `tools/`.
- `dental_clinic.db` is committed (local SQLite seed) and intentionally tracked.

Tests use an **in-memory SQLite** DB seeded by `tests/conftest.py` — no real DB needed. The `client` fixture overrides `get_db` and seeds the `default` clinic; `client_market_mall` additionally seeds the `market-mall-denture` clinic (providers 101/102, busy blocks, service 700).

DB migration for existing PG instances:
```bash
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://... python scripts/migrate_add_clinics.py
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://... python scripts/migrate_clinic_contact_fields.py
```

Deployment target is Google Cloud Run — see `DEPLOY_GOOGLE_CLOUD.md`. Cloud Run requires the Unix-socket DSN form: `postgresql://USER:PASS@/DB?host=/cloudsql/PROJECT:REGION:INSTANCE`.

## Architecture

**Single FastAPI app (`api/main.py`) with SQLAlchemy ORM.** The DB is the source of truth; there is no external calendar sync (despite the `calendar_event_id` column and `/api/calendar/*` endpoint names — these are vestigial naming).

### Multi-tenancy (critical)

Every request is scoped by clinic via the `X-Clinic-Id` header (default: `"default"`). This is enforced by the `get_clinic` dependency (`api/main.py:43`), which 404s if the clinic row doesn't exist. **Every query in the API filters by `clinic_id`** — when adding endpoints or queries, you must scope by `clinic.id` or you'll leak data across tenants. Per-clinic config (timezone, working hours, address, contact phone, booking notification email) lives on the `Clinic` row, not in env vars.

Tests reflect this: the `client` fixture seeds the `default` clinic before any request can succeed.

### Slot computation

`tools/slot_utils.get_available_slots` computes availability **from the DB** by intersecting:
- Per-clinic working hours (`hour_start`, `hour_end`) and timezone
- `ProviderBusyBlock` rows (recurring weekly *unavailable* windows — note: busy blocks mean UNAVAILABLE, not available)
- Existing `Appointment` rows with status in `{SCHEDULED, CONFIRMED, PENDING_SYNC, PENDING}`

The same "active statuses" set is used for conflict detection in create/reschedule (`api/main.py:319`, `api/main.py:916`). When changing status semantics, update both the slot computation and the conflict checks together.

### Background notifications

`POST /api/calendar/events`, `PUT /appointments/{id}/cancel`, and `PUT /appointments/{id}/reschedule` schedule **FastAPI `BackgroundTasks`** (in-process, not a queue) for:
- Twilio SMS to the patient (`clients/sms_client.py`) — toggled by `SEND_BOOKING_SMS`, delayed by `SMS_DELAY_SECONDS`.
- SMTP email to the clinic on new bookings (`clients/email_client.py`) — recipient resolved by `resolve_booking_notification_recipient`: `BOOKING_NOTIFICATION_TO` env var overrides the per-clinic `clinic.booking_notification_email` (use the env var only for testing; leave it unset in prod).

Notifications must never raise back to the request handler — they're best-effort and logged on failure.

### Database connection

`database/connection.py` resolves `DATABASE_URL` in this priority order: `POSTGRES_URL` → `POSTGRES_PRISMA_URL` → `POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING` → `DATABASE_URL` → `sqlite:////tmp/dental_clinic.db`. It also normalizes `postgres://` → `postgresql://` and strips Supabase-only `supa=` query params. `init_db()` is called from the FastAPI lifespan, so tables are created on startup; failures are logged but don't crash the app.

### Resilient entrypoint

`run_api.py` always binds to `PORT` even if `api.main` fails to import — it falls back to a stub app that only serves `/health`. This is intentional so Cloud Run / Railway health checks pass and you can debug a misconfigured deploy via logs rather than a crash loop. Don't "fix" this by removing the try/except.

## Design System (mandatory for all frontend/UI work)

**Before writing, modifying, or reviewing any frontend code** (`frontend/src/`, HTML prototypes, marketing pages), you MUST read and follow the Rockyridge Dental AI design system:

1. Read `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai-dental.com/SKILL.md` — it defines when and how to apply the brand.
2. Read `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai-dental.com/README.md` — brand voice, component library, file layout.
3. Read `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai-dental.com/colors_and_type.css` — all design tokens live here. Never invent new colours, radii, or font sizes.
4. Browse `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai-dental.com/preview/` for component markup references.
5. Check `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai-dental.com/ui_kits/website/` for full-page compositions.

Key rules:
- Use `--primary` / `--foreground` / `--border` CSS custom properties — never raw hex values in components.
- Light theme for all clinical surfaces. Dark theme is only for the login portal.
- No emoji, no filled icons, no playful/spring easing.
- Production React code imports from `frontend/src/components/ui/` (which already consumes these tokens). Never import from the `design_system/` folder at runtime.
- Brand voice: authoritative, clinical, calm. Definite article on system parts (The Schedule, The Roster, The Lab).

## Things to know

- **Ignore existing styling in `frontend/src/`.** The current design/CSS in the frontend app is not authoritative. Always follow the Design System section above — do not preserve or match the existing look and feel in `frontend/src/`.
- **Ignore `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai.com/` and `frontend/design_system/dental-pms.v1/`.** Only use `frontend/design_system/rockyridgeai-dental.com/` as the design source.
- Provider was renamed from "Doctor"; the `Provider` model is generic (denturist, doctor, assistant, etc.) and `provider.title` + `provider.name` are joined for display (`"Dr Smith"`).
- Default timezone fallback throughout is `America/Edmonton`.
- `tmp/` is an archive of older docs/frontend/scripts — don't add new code there.
- The empty `services/` directory is a leftover; business logic currently lives in `api/main.py` and `tools/`.
- `dental_clinic.db` is committed (local SQLite seed) and intentionally tracked.

Now I have everything I need. Let me formulate the implementation plan.

---

**Implementation Plan — Full-Page Appointment Workspace (Design System Prototype)**

**Problem Statement:**
When clicking an existing appointment card on the dashboard, the current behavior opens a simple `DetailDrawer` (right-side panel with key-value rows). The goal is to replace this with a full-page overlay workspace focused on the selected appointment, providing a compact clinical view with appointment header, tooth chart, and status action buttons. This is design system prototype work only (`dashboard.html`).

**Requirements:**
- Clicking an appointment card opens a full-page overlay (not a drawer) focused on that appointment
- The workspace shows: appointment header (patient avatar + name + time + procedure + status), tooth chart, and status action buttons (Confirm, Check-in, Start, Complete, No-show, Cancel, Reschedule)
- Transition: fade + slide up (400ms ease-out per design system motion tokens)
- "Back to Dashboard" button: top-left arrow + text label
- Design system prototype only — `dashboard.html` in `ui_kits/website/`
- Follow all design system tokens from `colors_and_type.css` — no raw hex outside existing token values
- Brand voice: authoritative, clinical, calm. No emoji.

**Background:**
- The dashboard currently uses `setDetail(...)` → `DetailDrawer` for appointment card clicks
- The `DetailDrawer` is a generic right-side drawer with key-value rows — also used for patients, invoices, and lab cases
- The `ToothChartTile` component already exists in the design system with FDI numbering and status fills
- The `AppointmentCard` component provides the appointment data shape: `{ id, time, duration, patient_id, patient, provider, chair, kind, status }`
- Status tones are already defined in `AppointmentCard.jsx`: confirmed (green), pending (amber), no_show (red), completed (grey)
- Design system motion: `--duration-slow: 400ms`, `--ease-out: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`
- The `drawer-back` CSS class was already added in the previous change set

**Proposed Solution:**
1. Add CSS for the full-page workspace overlay (`.appt-workspace`, animation keyframes)
2. Create an `AppointmentWorkspace` component that renders the full-page view for a selected appointment
3. Change the `Dashboard` component to use `setActiveAppt(appointment)` instead of `setDetail(...)` for appointment card clicks, rendering `AppointmentWorkspace` when an appointment is selected
4. Keep `DetailDrawer` intact for patients, invoices, and lab cases — only appointment cards change behavior

**Task Breakdown:**

Task 1: Add CSS for the full-page appointment workspace
- Clear objective: Add CSS rules for `.appt-workspace` (full-page overlay), `.appt-ws-header`, `.appt-ws-body`, `.appt-ws-actions`, and a `@keyframes wsEnter` animation (fade + slide up, 400ms)
- Implementation: Insert after the existing `.drawer-back:hover` rule block, before `/* Toast */`. Use design system tokens (`--ease-out`, `--duration-slow`, `--rr-warm-white`, `--rr-parchment`, etc.). The workspace is `position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: 40; background: var(--rr-warm-white)` with the slide-up animation.
- Test: Visual — open dashboard.html, confirm no CSS errors, workspace styles are defined.
- Demo: CSS loads without errors; no visual change yet since no component uses the classes.

Task 2: Create the `AppointmentWorkspace` component
- Clear objective: Build a React component that receives an appointment object and `onClose` callback, rendering a full-page overlay with: (1) a top bar with back-arrow + "Back to Dashboard" label, (2) an appointment header showing patient avatar/initials, patient name, appointment ID, time, duration, procedure, provider, operatory, and status badge, (3) the `ToothChartTile` component, and (4) a row of status action buttons (Confirm, Check-in, Start, Complete, No-show, Cancel, Reschedule).
- Implementation: Insert the component definition before the `Dashboard` component in the `<script type="text/babel">` block. Use the existing `drawer-back` class for the back button. Reuse the status tone map from `AppointmentCard.jsx` for the status badge. The tooth chart is the existing `<ToothChartTile />`. Action buttons use `btn btn-primary btn-md` and `btn btn-ghost btn-md` classes. ESC key closes the workspace.
- Test: Visual — component renders correctly when called with a sample appointment.
- Demo: The `AppointmentWorkspace` component exists and can be rendered.

Task 3: Wire appointment card clicks to the workspace and keep DetailDrawer for other entities
- Clear objective: In the `Dashboard` component, add `activeAppt` state. Change the `AppointmentCard` `onClick` to `setActiveAppt(a)` instead of `setDetail(...)`. Render `{activeAppt && <AppointmentWorkspace appointment={activeAppt} onClose={() => setActiveAppt(null)} />}` alongside the existing `detail && <DetailDrawer ...>` (which still handles patients, invoices, lab cases).
- Implementation: Add `const [activeAppt, setActiveAppt] = React.useState(null);` to Dashboard state. Replace the `onClick` in the `AppointmentCard` map. Add the conditional render near the other overlays.
- Test: Visual — click an appointment card, full-page workspace appears with fade+slide-up. Click "Back to Dashboard", returns. Click a patient card or invoice row, the old DetailDrawer still works.
- Demo: Full end-to-end flow: click appointment → workspace opens with animation → see patient info, tooth chart, action buttons → click back → dashboard returns. Other cards (patients, invoices, lab) still open the DetailDrawer.

Does this plan look good, or would you like me to adjust anything?
go ahead and execute it
